name: المستقبل — Future Tense with سـ / سوف

cards.grammarNote: The future is formed by prefixing سَـ (sa-) directly to the present tense verb: سَـ + أَعْمَلُ = سَأَعْمَلُ. سَـ is short and informal; سَوْفَ (sawfa) is the formal/emphatic alternative: سَوْفَ أَعْمَل.
سَـ + تَدْرُسُ (she studies) = سَتَدْرُسُ (she will study). The present tense verb form is identical to the future — only the سَـ prefix changes the meaning to future.
To form a yes/no question, add هَلْ (hal) before the verb: هَلْ سَتَأْتِي؟ The verb سَتَأْتِي = you will come (2nd person m. singular). هَلْ never changes.
لَنْ (lan) = will not (emphatic future negation). It is followed by the subjunctive: أَذْهَبَ (not أَذْهَبُ). Do not use سَـ with لَنْ — they cannot combine. لَنْ أَذْهَبَ = I will absolutely not go.
سَـ + يُسَافِرُ (he travels) = سَيُسَافِرُ (he will travel). الأُسْبُوعَ الْقَادِم = next week (the coming week), in the accusative as a time adverb.

cards.front: The company will open a new branch.
cards.grammarNote: سَوْفَ (sawfa) is the formal/written version of سَـ, used in news, official announcements, and formal speech. It is written as a separate word before the present-tense verb. Both mean 'will'.
لَنْ + subjunctive + أَبَدًا (abadan, never/ever) is used for strong emphatic future negation. لَنْ تَسْتَسْلِمَ = she will not surrender (subjunctive form of تَسْتَسْلِمُ).
